description: The Civil Service serves the government of the day and supports them to develop and implement its policies as effectively as possible.

links.title: Log in to find e-learning, online resources and classroom courses to support your Civil Service career

items.summary: Civil Service Learning provides courses and resources for developing skills common to all UK civil servants in all Civil Service roles.

This is for current and prospective members of the Civil Service pension arrangements. It also contains links to information for past members who are not yet receiving their pension.

There are also links for pensioners who are already receiving their Civil Service pension.

image.alt_text: Graphic showing diverse group of people in different professions

items.summary:
A career in the Civil Service offers you:

- the opportunity to make a difference to people’s lives in the UK
- learning on the job and good career development opportunities
- flexible working hours and comprehensive benefits

You can now explore careers across the Civil Service on the Civil Service Careers website.

ordered_promotional_features.title: Discover careers in the Civil Service

details.body:
The role of Permanent Secretary carries with it the responsibility of Principal Accounting Officer, ensuring that the resources authorised by Parliament are used for the purposes authorised by Parliament.

Other responsibilities include:

  • agreeing with the Home Secretary the overarching strategy for delivering ministerial objectives
  • leading, and managing the department to ensure that it operates as effectively and efficiently as possible
  • reporting to the Home Secretary and to Parliament on overall performance

role_appointments.title: Dame Antonia Romeo DCB - Permanent Secretary

details.body: The Cabinet Secretary is the Prime Minister’s most senior policy adviser and acts as Secretary to the Cabinet, responsible to all ministers for the running of Cabinet Government.

role_appointments.title: Dame Antonia Romeo DCB - Cabinet Secretary

details.body:
The Head of the Civil Service leads nearly half a million public servants who work in public institutions, administer tax, benefits and pensions systems and put government policy into practice. The civil service is a permanent, politically impartial workforce that serves the government of the day, while retaining the flexibility to serve future governments.

Currently civil servants are supporting the government’s economic and public service reform. The scale of the challenges and persistent weaknesses require a reform plan that applies right across the civil service. The Head of the Civil Service is one of several senior civil servants accountable for the reform of the civil service through the Civil Service Board.

role_appointments.title: Dame Antonia Romeo DCB - Head of the Civil Service

details.body:
The role of Permanent Secretary carries with it the responsibility of Principal Accounting Officer, ensuring that the resources authorised by Parliament are used for the purposes authorised by Parliament.

Other responsibilities include:

  • agreeing with the Secretary of State the overarching strategy for delivering Ministerial objectives
  • leading, and managing the Ministry to ensure that it operates as effectively and efficiently as possible
  • reporting to the Justice Secretary and to Parliament on overall performance

role_appointments.title: Dame Antonia Romeo DCB - Permanent Secretary

details.body: The Permanent Secretary is responsible for leading the Department for International Trade, and is the Secretary of State’s principal policy adviser.

The Director-General Economic and Commercial Affairs USA sits on the Board of Department for International Trade, the foreign commercial arm of the UK government, and leads its operations across the USA.

role.title: Director-General Economic and Commercial Affairs USA

role_appointments.title: Antonia Romeo - Director-General Economic and Commercial Affairs USA

details.body:
The Consul General is the senior UK official in a Consulate General, which is a subordinate office to the Embassy or High Commission, usually located in another major city. The Consul General represents the UK government and is typically responsible for consular, visa and trade activities in their city or region.

The Consul General leads the government’s work promoting UK economic, commercial, political and consular interests in New York, Pennsylvania, New Jersey and Fairfield County, Connecticut.

British Consulate General New York

role.title: British Consul General to New York

role_appointments.title: Antonia Romeo - British Consul General in New York

role.title: Government's Special Envoy to the U.S. technology companies

role_appointments.title: Antonia Romeo - Government's Special Envoy to the U.S. technology companies

details.body:
The Director General heads the Economic and Domestic Secretariat in the Cabinet Office, which supports the Prime Minister and the Cabinet in taking forward the government’s economic and domestic priorities.

The Director General is also responsible for ensuring that Government decisions are based on the best possible data and strategic analysis, working with colleagues across the Cabinet Secretariat, No.10, all departments, and external experts, and by facilitating collective decision-making through Cabinet and its committees.

role.title: Director General, Economic and Domestic Secretariat

role_appointments.title: Antonia Romeo - Director General, Economic and Domestic Secretariat

details.body:
The group led by the Director General, Criminal Justice has responsibility for:

  • CJS policy and strategy
  • Victim and criminal proceedings policy
  • CJS services (including for victims)
  • CJS Reform projects
  • Sentencing and penalties
  • Rehabilitation
  • Offender policy
  • Women offenders
  • Youth justice
  • MoJ strategy
  • Civil Service Reform, including open/agile policy making
  • Structures and Services Review (SSR)

role_appointments.title: Antonia Romeo - Director General, Criminal Justice

details.body: The Cabinet Secretary is the Prime Minister’s most senior policy adviser and acts as Secretary to the Cabinet, responsible to all ministers for the running of Cabinet Government.
